reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff of April 29, 2025, President Alassane Ouattara has not officially declared his candidacy for the 2025 election. While he hinted in January 2025 that he was in 'good health' and 'eager to continue serving,' he explicitly stated at that time that he had not yet made a formal decision. This follows his 2020 pattern where he waited until August (Independence Eve) to declare, despite intense pressure from his party (the RHDP), which has considered him its 'natural candidate' since mid-2024. Although he declared earlier in 2015 (late April), the lack of a similar announcement or a scheduled nominating convention in May 2025 suggests he is likely to wait until the summer to maintain political suspense and flexibility. Reports through late April 2025 describe him as 'tight-lipped' and indicate that the official nomination process has not yet reached the acceptance stage.
